header.title: Tooltip
header.subtitle: A floating label that appears on hover or keyboard focus, providing brief contextual information about an element. Renders in a portal to avoid clipping by overflow containers.

anatomy.title: Anatomy
anatomy.description: The Tooltip wraps its child in an inline span. The floating content is portalled to document.body and positioned with fixed coordinates.

parts.wrapper: Inline span that captures hover and focus events.
parts.content: The floating tooltip element, rendered in a portal with role=tooltip.
parts.child: The element that triggers the tooltip. Receives aria-describedby pointing to the tooltip.

sides.title: Sides
sides.description: The tooltip can appear on any of the four sides of the trigger.

delay.title: Delay
delay.description: The delay prop controls how many milliseconds to wait before showing the tooltip. Useful to avoid flashing tooltips during fast mouse traversal.

focus.title: Keyboard focus
focus.description: Tooltips also appear when the trigger receives focus — important for keyboard and screen reader users.

props.content: The text or node to display inside the tooltip.
props.side: Which side of the trigger the tooltip appears on.
props.delay: Milliseconds to wait before showing the tooltip.
props.className: Additional classes applied to the tooltip container.
props.extendsNote: Tooltip does not extend native element props. The child element receives aria-describedby automatically.

limitations.touch: Touch devices do not trigger hover events. The tooltip is only visible on devices that support hover or keyboard focus. This is acceptable for supplemental labels — never use a tooltip as the only source of critical information.
